text.title: ab-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ab-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Produces a different variant on every page load, request, or process restart. The same user alternates between variants, cross-contaminating both groups and making results meaningless.

failure-mode.description: Running successive experiments on the same population without changing the hash salt (experiment ID).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Correlated assignment: users in control for experiment 1 are always in control for experiment 2. This produces correlated bias that looks like real effects.

failure-mode.description: Stopping the experiment the moment the p-value crosses 0.05 (peeking).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple comparisons over time inflate the false-positive rate. Stopping on first significance yields a 20–30% false-positive rate instead of the intended 5%.

failure-mode.description: Using `scipy.stats.ttest_ind` on a binary (0/1) conversion metric.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The t-test assumes approximately normal data. Binary metrics violate this for small or imbalanced samples, producing incorrect p-values. Use chi-square or two-proportion z-test.

failure-mode.description: Logging conversions without logging exposures, then computing conversion rate as conversions / total_users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users not exposed to the experiment are included in the denominator, diluting the conversion rate and obscuring real effects. Denominator must be unique exposed users per variant.
